titleStmt.title: SII 16.178: original edition by H. K. Narasimhaswami and G. S. Gai
No. 178. (A.R. No. 373 of 1926.) TAṄGĒḌA, PALNAD TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.

summary.p: On a slab built into the wall in front of the Hanumān temple near the fort-gate in the village.
This is dated Śaka 1474 (current), Virōdhikṛit, Āshāḍha śu. 15, Thursday, corresponding to 1551 A.D., June 18.

It records the exemption of the taxes such as jātipannu, aśivechcham, āṭapannu, etc., on the barber community of Taṅgēḍa as well as Taṅgēḍa-sīma, made by Daḷavāyi Veṅgaḷa-nāyaka on the orders of Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Rāmarāju Rāmayyadēva-Mahārṇju at the request of the barbers Koṇḍōju and Timmōju.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
© svastiśrī jayābhyudaya śālivāhana śakava
¨rṣaṁbulu 1474 Agunēṁṭi virōdhakṛt saṁvva
tsara Āṣāḍa śu 15 gu . śrīmanmahārājādhirāja rā
japaramēśvara śrī vīrapratāpa śrī sadāśivadēvama
hārāyalu vidyānagaramaṁddu vajraśiṁhvāsanā
sīnulai pṛthivirājyaṁ sēyucuṁḍḍaṁggānu . sa
dāśivamahārāyala rājyabhāradhuraṁdharu
layina . śrīmanmahāmaṁḍḍalēśvara rāmarāju rā
mayyadēva mahārājulu tirumalayyadēva ma
hārājulaṁgāri nirūpāla kramāna daḷavāya veṁ
ggaḷnāyiniṁgāru taṁggeḍa taṁggeḍaśīma maṁggaḷavoju
laku yiccina śāsanamu . rāmarājayyavāriki
nni tirumalrājayyavārikinni maṁggaḷla koṁḍḍōjunnu
tiṁmmōjuṁnnu manavi vinnapaṁ ccēsukoni rāya
rājyānaṁ galigina maṁggaḷlavāru Aṁdarikinni jāti
pannuveṭṭi sahā mānpiṁcci Anniśīmalānu śāsanālu
veyyiṁccinārugnā . rājula nirūpāla kramāna mīru
nnu jātipannu suṁkhyāḷitaṁ prajalalō Aśiveccaṁ Āṭapannu
sahā rokhālunnu veṭṭinni yallappaṭikinni sa¨rva
mā(ṁ)nyaṁ sēstimignā . mī kulakāyakaṁ sē
sukoni mīku naḍacē kaṭṭagasavulu jātiva
¨rtana svāṁmyālu Anubhaviṁcci pariṇāmāna vuṁ
ḍēdi . yī mā(ṁ)nyānaku yivvaru tappinānu gā
ḍida kaḍupuvaṁ buṭṭuvāru gaṁggaka¨rtanu gō
vu brāṁhmani vadhiyiṁcina pappāna bōvuvāRead pāpāna
ru śunaka māṁsyaṁ tinnavāru . śrī .
Digital edition of SII 16.178 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
